content: cap-ai-provider: Vercel AI SDK implementations (createAIService, CostEstimator, AIResponseCache, resolveLanguageModel) live in capabilities/cap-ai-provider, not in core. Core retains AIService interface, createNoopAIService, AIBoundary, PromptSanitizer, OutputValidator. Consumers import createAIService from @linchkit/cap-ai-provider.

description: Merge conflict failed at tier ai-resolve. Branch: overstory/builder-derived-split/linchkit-09e2. Agent: builder-derived-split. Conflicting files: packages/core/src/schema/derived-property.ts.

name: multi-agent-review-gate
description: multi-review gate: .overstory/scripts/multi-review.sh integrates Gemini + Codex review before merge_ready; dual mode for small changes, triple for >5 files or >200 lines; exit 0=pass, 1=fail, 2=error

content: ChatterPanel replaces ActivityPanel in schema-form.tsx detail tabs (Spec 53). ChatterPanel is a UI-only component in cap-adapter-ui-react that connects to @linchkit/cap-chatter GraphQL API. Falls back gracefully (empty state) when cap-chatter not installed. Uses queryChatterMessages + addChatterMessage in api.ts. Subscription via useSubscription hook with onChatterMessage GraphQL subscription.

name: force-merge-recovery
description: Force merge worktree failure recovery: when builder-merge fails in worktree, re-execute merges directly in main repo (feat/m2). Resolve .mulch/mulch.config.yaml conflicts with --theirs (worktree config is stale). Stash uncommitted changes first.

content: EventHandlerContext interface should only expose emit(); execute/get/query were dead stubs never called by any handler. createHandlerContext in EventBus and OutboxWorker differ only in emit behavior (fire-and-forget vs swallow).
Always import from @linchkit/core or @linchkit/core/server (the entry points), never from deep sub-paths like @linchkit/core/utils/env — those are internal module paths, not public API.

tsconfig paths must include all @linchkit/core subpath exports (e.g. @linchkit/core/config, @linchkit/core/utils/env) to prevent worktree isolation from resolving them to canonical node_modules, which causes ConfigRegistry type mismatches across module boundaries

Elysia route handler set.status has type 'status?: number | string' (optional), so shared helper functions that accept the set object must use { status?: number | string | undefined } not { status: number | string | undefined } to avoid TypeScript assignability errors
